channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Survivors still being found from Myanmar earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue workers saved a 63-year-old woman from the rubble of a building in Myanmar's capital on Tuesday, but hope was fading of finding many more survivors of the violent earthquake that killed more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Amsterdam knife attacker who injured 2 Americans suspected of having 'terrorist motive,' prosecutors say
item.description: The Ukrainian suspect who stabbed five people in Amsterdam last week, including two Americans, likely had a "terrorist motive," Dutch prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrat warns of ‘catastrophic’ consequences to Trump tariffs
item.description: Sen. Ed Markey (D-Mass.) warned Tuesday of “catastrophic” consequences to President Trump’s tariff plans.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: EU prepared to retaliate against Trump tariffs, von der Leyen says
item.description: The European Union (EU) is prepared to retaliate against President Trump’s existing and upcoming tariffs, although the trade bloc is open to negotiations with Washington, according to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: More Americans see permanent Gaza ceasefire as priority: Survey
item.description: More Americans now see a permanent ceasefire in Gaza as a priority, according to a new The Associated Press-NORC Research Center poll.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Here's a look at the election ban on France's far-right Le Pen and the legal issues
item.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has denounced the court ruling that placed a five-year ban on her seeking public office for embezzlement.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Survivors still being found from Burma earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: Burma was hit by a 7.7 magnitude earthquake on Friday, killing more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war. Thailand also sustained damage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE: University of Minnesota student's visa not revoked for protests
item.description: The University of Minnesota international graduate student detained last week by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) had his visa revoked over a drunk driving case, not due to participation in pro-Palestinian protests on campus, according to a senior Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Pope's work during convalescence includes clearing path for saints from Venezuela, Papua New Guinea
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis spends part of his days at his desk working and concelebrates Mass daily in his private chapel.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Germany says US talks on Russia-Ukraine war are 'deadlocked,' but China has different take
item.description: German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock says U.S. talks on Russia-Ukraine war are in a "deadlock," while Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi says the parties are making progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A fireball from a burst gas pipeline in Malaysia injures 145 people
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — A fireball that erupted from a burst gas pipeline soared into the sky outside Malaysia's largest city and injured 145 people as it burned for several hours before being put out, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Marine Le Pen brought the far right to France’s front door
item.description: PARIS (AP) — For years, Marine Le Pen stood at the gates of power — poised, relentless and rising. She stripped the French far right of its old symbols, sanded down its roughest edges and built in its place a sleek, disciplined machine with the single goal of winning the country's presidency.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk slams Le Pen ruling, says it will 'backfire' like Trump's as some on global right face legal troubles
item.description: Marine Le Pen’s sentencing in France has drawn global attention, with parallels made to legal battles faced by President Donald Trump, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, among other right-wing politicians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany sees Ukraine truce efforts as deadlocked, while China says the talks are encouraging
item.description: Germany’s chief diplomat is describing U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to secure a truce in the three-year war between Russia and Ukraine as deadlocked.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Volcano begins erupting in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town and spa are evacuated
item.description: Iceland’s Met Office says a volcano is erupting in the southwestern part of the country, hours after a nearby town and a geothermal spa were evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan's Ishiba says he will push for US auto tariffs exemption
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba said Tuesday his government is making last ditch efforts to get U.S. President Donald Trump to exclude his country from an increase in auto tariffs due to take effect from Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangkok reviews construction safety after a high-rise was crumpled by an earthquake 800 miles away
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai authorities said Monday they are investigating why a single office tower block under construction in Bangkok collapsed during Friday's earthquake that otherwise caused limited damage in the capital. Bangkok Gov.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas terrorists torture protester to death in Gaza, leave his body on family’s doorstep: report
item.description: A Palestinian protester opposed to Hamas' rule over the Gaza Strip was beaten and tortured to death following a demonstration last week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French court finds right-wing leader Marine Le Pen guilty of embezzlement
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A French court found Marine Le Pen guilty on Monday in an embezzlement case but didn’t immediately say what her sentence might be and how it might impact the right-wing leader’s political future.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces order evacuation for most of Rafah ahead of attack on the area
item.description: The Israeli military issued evacuation orders on Monday for most of Rafah and suggested it may soon launch another ground operation in the city.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600 and leaves countless buried
item.description: MANDALAY, Myanmar (AP) — The smell of decaying bodies permeated the streets of Myanmar's second-largest city on Sunday as people worked frantically by hand to clear rubble in the hope of finding someone still alive, two days after a massive earthquake struck that killed more than 1,600 people and le
item.dc:creator: THEIN ZAW, DAVID RISING and GRANT PECK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran unless they end nuclear weapons program and begin talks on new deal
item.description: Trump warned Iran after sending a letter to revive nuclear talks. With "maximum pressure" back on the table, tensions between the two nations continue to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Landmark UK report on Hamas massacre exposes worst attack on Jews since Holocaust
item.description: A UK Parliament report chaired by respected historian Lord Andrew Roberts calls the Oct. 7 Hamas attack in Israel deliberate and brutal, urging democracies to combat denial and misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: Charles' cancer diagnosis has heaped pressure on the British monarchy, which is still evolving after the 70-year reign of the late Queen Elizabeth II.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
